verdict: leading beyond-2nd-cumulant DIFFERENCE common-mode suppressed by a0 (<=0.096 at endpoint); envelope Delta F^(2)(1-a0)>0; all-order common-mode is the residual

claims.name: dressing_pattern_independent
claims.detail: (r_hat(I)=rR+2 lam' I depends only on I, NOT on the pattern (P or R_H): the common dressing D_0(I) is shared, so the beyond-2nd-cumulant common-mode part CANCELS in the difference -- the near-gap R-U10-3 mechanism extended to the bulk)

claims.name: a0_controlled_at_endpoint
claims.detail: (a0(2e-3)=0.0957 < 0.3: the condensate carries only ~10% of the dressing, so the pattern-dependent (non-cancelling) beyond-2nd-cumulant difference is O(a0)-suppressed)

claims.name: leading_envelope_positive
claims.detail: (Delta F_exact >~ Delta F^(2)(1-a0) with (1-a0)=0.979/0.950/0.904 > 0.5 across the operating interval: the leading common-mode envelope keeps the selection margin positive)

claims.name: a0_monotone_worst_at_endpoint
claims.detail: (a0 monotone increasing in I: worst at the I=2e-3 endpoint (a0=0.0957), consistent with the RES-4 / SC-SCOPE endpoint being the thinnest)
